% Lablog fixture: física con physics/braket/siunitx (PDF + macros KaTeX parciales)

\section*{Mecánica cuántica}
\[
\hat{H}\ket{\psi} = E\ket{\psi},\quad
\braket{\phi}{\psi},\quad
\ketbra{\psi}{\psi},\quad
\bra{\psi}\hat{O}\ket{\psi}
\]
\[
\left[ \hat{x}, \hat{p} \right] = i\hbar,\quad
U(t) = \exp\!\left(-\frac{i\hat{H}t}{\hbar}\right)
\]

\section*{Electromagnetismo}
\[
\nabla\cdot\mathbf{E} = \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0},\quad
\nabla\times\mathbf{B} - \mu_0\varepsilon_0\frac{\partial\mathbf{E}}{\partial t} = \mu_0\mathbf{J}
\]
\[
F^{\mu\nu} = \partial^\mu A^\nu - \partial^\nu A^\mu,\quad
\mathcal{L} = -\frac{1}{4}F_{\mu\nu}F^{\mu\nu} + \bar{\psi}(i\gamma^\mu D_\mu - m)\psi
\]

\section*{Unidades y números}
Longitud: $\ell = \SI{12.3}{\milli\meter}$,
energía: $E = \SI{1.5e3}{\electronvolt}$,
constante: $c = \SI{2.99792458e8}{\meter\per\second}$.

\section*{Derivadas (physics)}
\[
\dv{f}{x},\quad
\pdv{f}{x},\quad
\pdv[2]{f}{x},\quad
\dv{t}\begin{pmatrix} x \\ p \end{pmatrix}
= \begin{pmatrix} \partial H/\partial p \\ -\partial H/\partial x \end{pmatrix}
\]
